quote_of_day.text: I still don't understand why Animal was hated so much. I enjoyed it more than Dhurandhar, Animal had more literally me moments with that swastika scene. Animal feels more timeless, and a very very very good story on toxic father etc situations. It was a fantastic movie
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A toxic high pressure system of anti-Sub-Saharan African sentiment dominated the board, sparking controversy and drawing squalls of replies.

This oppressive front brought with it a mass of vitriolic comments, including one that likened Sub-Saharan Africans to having '0 impulse control like a homo erectus.' A critical mass of dissent accumulated around India's leadership and development, manifesting as a low-pressure system of frustration.

Anon criticised the country's priorities, suggesting that energy was being squandered on 'non-stop bhangi reservation scams, caste votebank warfare and "hisab chukta" politics' rather than constructive pursuits like manufacturing and R&D.

Meanwhile, a fog of speculation shrouded the sudden departure of 'Bharatchan,' with one anon seeking answers and cautioning against hate.

In other parts of the board, isolated incidents of provocative statements occurred, including a call for British colonization of India and a claim that opinions from non-blue states carried no weight.

These incidents represented scattered but intense weather events.

articles.title: Anon Poster Sparks Controversy with Anti-Sub-Saharan African Remarks
articles.body:
An anonymous poster sparked controversy on an Indian imageboard with a comment about the "Chaddi mein coom nikal gaya mera darr ke maare" and described people from sub-Saharan Africa as having "0 impulse control like a homo erectus." One respondent, Anon, referenced a documentary about a Russian alliance in the Central African Republic war and claimed that "nigger leaders are often incompetent af" and "corrupt and have said outrageous shit and have taken dumb decisions." Another Anon expressed a desire to see Candace Owens, an American conservative commentator, in a blue saree, cosplaying as a Dalit girl and chanting "Jai Bhim" slogans.

This poster also fantasized about having sex with her. Anon also mentioned that Israel's actions against subhumanoids are "top notch" and referenced the Entebbe raid, where Israelis rescued their citizens from Uganda.

The poster claimed that Israelis "rape nations for less" and "get away with anything." The conversation took a darker turn with Anon suggesting that people from sub-Saharan Africa are "not even human beings" and that their genetic makeup is only one-third sapiens.

This poster also claimed that if not for Jewish influence, most races would agree on "total negro death." Anon discussed the idea of keeping a "personal ebony servant" for sexual purposes, as long as they are kept sterile and do not produce "half niglets" that could contaminate their race.

The thread also touched on the topic of intelligence, with Anon stating that people from sub-Saharan Africa have an average IQ of 80, which they claimed is the reason for their "pathetic state" and poor governance.

The conversation remained highly divisive and featured numerous racist and inflammatory comments.

articles.title: Criticism of India's Leadership and Development
articles.body:
Anon criticized India's leaders, saying they spend too much energy on scams and votebank politics rather than building factories, manufacturing, and R&D. This, according to Anon, is why India remains a laughingstock, still importing everything.

Another anon responded, suggesting that if India's leaders stopped certain divisive actions, such as "Varna is by choice" rhetoric, talented individuals from various ethnic groups might not abandon the nation.

This anon warned that if things continue this way, people may quit both the nation and its dominant faith within a decade.

One anon proposed a different perspective, suggesting that if India were to pause certain unproductive schemes, it could potentially save up to 100B$ and allocate it towards modernizing its military or addressing infrastructure issues like cleaning the Yamuna river.

The discussion also touched on India's military capabilities, with an anon stating that the country needs to modernize and expand its military, specifically to 13 Armoured Divisions, to be capable of sustained offensive operations.

This, however, would require significant funding, estimated at around 100B$.

Anon also expressed frustration with the current state of the nation, stating that India's politics revolves around appeasing certain groups, leading to a lack of aesthetic and developmental progress.

This sentiment was echoed by another anon, who described India as a "shithole" that will remain underdeveloped due to its focus on appeasement rather than progress.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on the Sudden Departure of 'Bharatchan'
articles.body:
Anon questioned what happened to 'bharatchan' and why they were referred to as 'gayush dalit.' The original poster also asked for information about the board's owner.

Anon provided an explanation, stating that the previous administrator, 'bharatchan,' had issues with hosting and reports, as well as internal conflicts and raids from other communities.

According to this anon, 'bharatchan' had been subtly signaling their departure for months due to the stress of managing the board. Another anon mentioned that 'bharatchan' had been doxxed, which contributed to their departure.

The doxxing occurred when 'bharatchan' referenced a workplace conversation and mentioned a religious figure and their last name, making it easy for others to identify them.

Anon discussed the consequences of the doxxing, including threats to email 'bharatchan's' Japanese workplace and potential deportation. One respondent expressed regret over the doxxing and apologized to 'bharatchan' if they were reading.

Anon also speculated about the identity of 'gayush,' with one suggesting that 'gayush' might be a foreign Muslim trying to incite civil war in India. Others discussed 'gayush's' possible connections to state-backed assets or their role in spreading hatred online.

The conversation also touched on the topic of another individual, 'motivanon,' who had been doxxed and had an Excel sheet of girls they had interacted with, which was used for harassment.

Anon debated the effectiveness of doxxing as a response to online actions. The discussion remained focused on the departure of 'bharatchan' and the speculation surrounding 'gayush' and other individuals, with no clear resolution or definitive answers.

articles.title: Anon Calls for British Colonization of India
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread by expressing a wish for the British to return and establish a "pure EUROPEAN AND WESTERN meritocracy" in India, calling Indians "shit lovers" and "low iq insects." Anon responded to the OP, questioning the group's manifesto and ideals, describing them as "retarded." Another Anon suggested that the movement would fail without proper organization.

The conversation turned to online security, with Übermensch discussing the limitations of ProtonMail's encryption and searching for alternative secure communication methods.

Anon also expressed support for a 50% women's reservation in parliament, while another Anon criticized the idea as undemocratic.

articles.title: Anon claims opinions from non-blue states don't matter on India
articles.body:
Anon started the thread, saying unless you're from a blue state, your opinions about India and its neighbors don't matter.

Another anon replied, suggesting that some Indian states, like Bihar, haven't developed an effective economic model despite having advantages like fertile plains and minerals.

A respondent argued that GDP per capita is not a meaningful metric, citing examples of states with high population but lower per capita GDP.

One anon joked that Mallu women are a significant contributor to certain communities, while another claimed that Kerala's economic performance is due to historically unfair policies and geography.
